tasks.name: chat
tasks.desc: Predict the next token of a message from a corpus of simple chat conversations (subtitles, SODA dialogue, short stories). Short dialogues = weak long-range structure, so it mostly tests local language modelling. Vocab ~16k.

tasks.name: wiki
tasks.desc: Predict the next token in SimpleWiki articles — REAL long-range structure within an article, so it tests actually using a long context window. Vocab 16384.

tasks.name: web
tasks.desc: Predict the next token in real web text the system fetched through its sandbox and FILTERED for long-range structure (keeps only text its own filter scores as helpful). Byte-level (vocab 256).

tasks.name: synth:recall
tasks.desc: Associative recall — the system poses this to ITSELF: it is shown (key,value) pairs, then queried with a key and must recall that key's value from earlier in the sequence (a pure memory test). Difficulty D grows the pair count, recall distance and vocab; random baseline ln(V).

tasks.name: synth:copy
tasks.desc: Copy (self-posed): reproduce an earlier block of tokens — holding and emitting a span from memory. Difficulty D grows the block length.

atoms.name: ksharp
atoms.what: kk = F.softmax(kk * F.softplus(self.{n}_tau), dim=-1)
atoms.why: The system invented this one itself: mid-run it diagnosed a specific weakness in its own designs and wrote a new primitive — NTM softmax key-sharpening / SDM sparse addressing — that shapes the keys/queries before they reach memory to address it.

atoms.name: read3
atoms.what: r1 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, qq); r1 = r1 * torch.rsqrt(r1.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6); r2 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, r1); r2 = r2 * torch.rsqrt(r2.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6); oo = oo + torch.einsum('bh
atoms.why: The system invented this one itself: mid-run it diagnosed a specific weakness in its own designs and wrote a new primitive — RMS inter-hop scaling — that reads from the memory state to address it.

atoms.name: surprise_gated_write
atoms.what: er = vv - torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk); sg = torch.sigmoid(self.{n}_a * er.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True)); M = M + (sg * er).unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "A$^2$RD: Agentic Autoregressive Diffusion for Long Video Consistency" (arXiv:2605.06924v1). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it writes to the memory state) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: interference_cleanup_read
atoms.what: r1 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, qq); rn = r1 * torch.rsqrt(r1.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6); r2 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, rn); oo = oo + r1 - self.{n}_l * r2
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "Algorithmic Analysis of Dense Associative Memory: Finite-Size Guarantees and Adv" (arXiv:2604.12811v1). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it reads from the memory state) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: sparse_key_selection
atoms.what: thr = kk.abs().topk(max(kk.shape[-1] // 4, 1), -1).values[..., -1:]; kk = kk * (kk.abs() >= thr).to(kk.dtype)
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "Mixture of Chapters: Scaling Learnt Memory in Transformers" (arXiv:2603.21096v1). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it shapes the keys/queries before they reach memory) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: usage_saturation_norm
atoms.what: us = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True); M = M * torch.rsqrt(1.0 + us).unsqueeze(-1)
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "Noisy Channel-Output Feedback Capacity of the Linear Deterministic Interference" (arXiv:1502.04649v6). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it writes to the memory state) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: channel_retention
atoms.what: M = torch.sigmoid(self.{n}_p) * M
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "Unforgettable Generalization in Language Models" (arXiv:2409.02228v1). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it writes to the memory state) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: novelty_gated_write
atoms.what: fam = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True); M = M + (vv / (1.0 + fam)).unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: Distilled from a paper the system read when its search stalled — "Fast Vocabulary Transfer for Language Model Compression" (arXiv:2402.09977v1). It grows its own primitive vocabulary by reading the literature for mechanisms it lacks, then minting this one (it writes to the memory state) to try in new designs.

atoms.name: double_delta
atoms.what: p1 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk); M = M + 0.5 * (vv - p1).unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2); p2 = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk); M = M + 0.5 * (vv - p2).unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that writes to the memory state, adapted from DeltaProduct / iterative delta-rule. Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: softnorm_read
atoms.what: rd = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, qq); oo = oo + rd * torch.rsqrt(rd.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6)
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that reads from the memory state, adapted from modern Hopfield (normalised sharp read). Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: gate_read
atoms.what: rd = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, qq); oo = oo + rd * torch.sigmoid(self.{n}_g(qq))
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that reads from the memory state, adapted from gated attention / output gating. Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: ntm_erase
atoms.what: ek = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk); M = M - ek.unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2); M = M + vv.unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that writes to the memory state, adapted from Neural Turing Machine (erase-then-add). Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: temp_read
atoms.what: oo = oo + self.{n}_t * torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, qq)
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that reads from the memory state, adapted from temperature-scaled attention. Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: householder
atoms.what: hk = torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, kk); M = M - 2.0 * hk.unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2); M = M + vv.unsqueeze(-1) * kk.unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: A hand-seeded starting primitive that writes to the memory state, adapted from RWKV-7 / householder generalized delta. Part of the initial alphabet the search composes on top of and mutates.

atoms.name: syn31_w252
atoms.what: M = M + (((((kk) * (qq))) * (((kk) - (qq))))).unsqueeze(-1) * (kk).unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it writes to the memory state; val 3.976).

atoms.name: syn15_w394
atoms.what: M = M + (vv).unsqueeze(-1) * (((((kk) + (vv))) * (self.{n}_l1(qq)))).unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it writes to the memory state; val 3.663).

atoms.name: syn64_w753
atoms.what: M = torch.sigmoid(self.{n}_r) * M; M = M + (((((kk) * (vv))) * ((qq) * torch.rsqrt((qq).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6)))).unsqueeze(-1) * (vv).unsqueeze(-2)
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it writes to the memory state; val 3.1).

atoms.name: syn110_r036
atoms.what: oo = oo + (((torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, self.{n}_s1 * (vv))) - (kk)))
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it reads from the memory state; val 3.074).

atoms.name: syn166_r237
atoms.what: oo = oo + (torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, torch.tanh(torch.sigmoid(qq))))
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it reads from the memory state; val 3.016).

atoms.name: syn145_r349
atoms.what: oo = oo + (torch.einsum('bhij,bhj->bhi', M, torch.sigmoid(((kk) * (qq)))))
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it reads from the memory state; val 2.857).

atoms.name: syn215_w327
atoms.what: M = M + (((qq) - (torch.sigmoid(vv)))).unsqueeze(-1) * (((vv) * torch.rsqrt((vv).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6)) * torch.rsqrt(((vv) * torch.rsqrt((vv).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6)).pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + 1e-6)).uns
atoms.why: The search WROTE this operation itself — every round it mutates its own primitive grammar to mint candidate memory mechanisms, and it kept this one because designs using it scored well (it writes to the memory state; val 0.615).

tasks.name: synth:recall:0
tasks.why: the system diagnosed that its designs were ignoring long-range context, and posed a memory task they cannot solve WITHOUT using the window

corpuses.name: web
corpuses.what: 9 windows of real web text kept for long_range_memory
corpuses.why: fetched real long-range text via the sandbox and kept ONLY windows its long-range-structure filter scored above 0.1882 (median 0.1882) — the local chat corpus lacks long-range structure

corpuses.name: synth (recall / copy)
corpuses.what: procedurally generated memory tasks — a fresh random instance every eval
corpuses.why: a task whose ONLY way to score is to use memory, so a memory architecture's value becomes measurable (unlike chat/wiki, where local prediction already scores well)

operator_human_task: A Pangram String contains all the alphabets at least once

position: The bootstrap floor is solid. The operator core is built + live (router + experts + judge + write + propose; ~97% verified-rate on what it poses). The field/recompute families are EXACT (deterministic writers, any diffic

The write-distill flywheel is live ([1.12]/[1.30]): a coder-student is wired before the 7B in the escalation ladder; verified 7B/student writes distill DOWN so escalation shrinks over time. Exit metric = per-rung escalat

The own-repo self-repair loop works end-to-end (generate defect → 7B-repair → execution-verify → human-gated propose, cross-verified) — BUT generated-defects "fixes" just restore the original = a capability DEMO/METRIC,

The ARITHMETIC NUMERIC WALL is RESOLVED — limbed.py decomposes into finite per-digit tables (each learned EXACTLY) + carry/borrow/shift composition → exact arbitrary-precision arithmetic + folds. The wall was unbounded-o

The MONOLITHIC WRITE/JUDGE WALL is RESOLVED in mechanism — don't write/judge a monolith: JUDGE = recompute(WRITE-expert) + compare, exact (1.0) for every execution-verifiable family covered. Remaining coverage is a scale

The JUDGE is a normal module; its general form is PERCEPTUAL — result → scalar, built by decomposition. The universal result representation is the SCREEN → the general judge = screen pixels → scalar = the agent loop's va

CAPABILITY and HANDOFF are anti-correlated — a better code LM is a worse code JUDGE (it learns bugs are likely too). Train the operator core toward handoff DIRECTLY (contrastive), not via next-byte capability.

The EXACT-writers CONVERGED — flat n_experts on the field families = correct exact-writer mastery, NOT a stall; unbounded growth needs a HARDER substrate ([1.20] external / own-repo), not more climbing on the mastered on

TRACK PIVOT (2026-06-22→24): the seat is built DIRECTLY as router + experts, with the 7B (Qwen2.5-Coder) as the biggest/most-expensive EXPERT — a code TOOL, not a trained-from-search core. The NN-search (old Stage 0) is

The operator-core constraint is COMPOSITION/decomposition-discovery — execution-as-judge is the path ([1.20] → vm_exec); the recompute-classes are tapped, so the forward is the execution/composition path ([[ouroborus-mis

Diverse non-transformer topologies break transformer-walled niches (the project's core bet, live) — the substrate escapes local minima via MAP-Elites QD + an EI surrogate ([[ouroborus-atom-search-live]]). ---

hypernet.directive: Go to itch.io, choose a game, and win it -- starting from a blank desktop.
hypernet.label: DIRECTIVE
hypernet.detail: gaps/leaves · Go to itch.io, choose a ga..

loop_version._doc: LOOP VERSION (Gabriel 2026-07-03: 'adopt an era based naming system... like most software versioning'). Semver: fix DEPLOYS bump MINOR (1.1 -> 1.2), small tweaks bump PATCH (1.2 -> 1.2.1), architectural/breaking changes bump MAJOR (-> 2.0, Gabriel's call). THE DEPLOYING SESSION bumps this file as part of every coordinated deploy (same commit as the deploy notes) + appends history. Displays read it verbatim (publisher payload loop_version + app panel).

history.commit: loop-close deploy: batch-of-1 registration FIX + gen_acc/n_pairs + register-on-master + current_eids (#00)
changes: FIX batch-of-1 registration (the novelty-streak loop-close blocker): a 1-spec batch falls back to a single job (FLAT result) but dispatch_batch marked pending batch:True -> the poll looped res['results']=[] -> ACCEPTED single mints NEVER registered -> the graph_feed demand re-fired forever, streak stuck at 0. Fix: treat a flat single result as one item (res.get('results') or [res]).
rank-2.5: gen_acc + n_pairs on demand_behaviors.jsonl (#03 yield-ranking) -- VERIFIED LIVE (graph_feed demand_node gen_acc 0.9451)
register-on-master SAVE: composed judges -> composed_masters.jsonl (judge coverage index; COUNT half = #02 era_metrics)
current_eids: STRUCTURED eid list on EVERY mint_status write (#02 durable display -- ends the 3x-recurring prose-parse breakage; notes become human-only) -- VERIFIED LIVE

HONEST: restart transiently DUPLICATED the daemon (no enforced singleton, per PROCESS_TOPOLOGY.md); caught + killed the old, single daemon 39632 confirmed. Loop-close still needs #03's sum-max drop + an idle GPU (day-shift OOM).

inc4b: the grow/rewire GRAPH SEARCH (typed dataflow DAG) integrated as a GATED numeric master-fallback -- discovers fan-out/branching BEYOND the linear chain (x^2+x via add(mul(x,x),x), x fanned out 3x, re-evals correct held-out); judge frontier skipped (no slowdown). MASTER-ONLY for now (a graph master = no mint; the graph's demand_node->mint follow-on connects #00's consume-contract next)
DIRECTIVE 6: pure_tools 55 -> 105 tools (#01 union hook, no breakage)
#00 load_atoms wires all -> this daemon restart deploys inc4b + the 105 tools
verified py_compile clean + #01 demo no-regression

Gabriel: minor version-bump (new loop capability)

roadmap_status._doc: Roadmap tracker data for the webpage 'roadmap' tab (Gabriel-directed). MAINTAINED BY #02 (planning-surface lane); RENDERED BY #01 (publish_web _plan payload + ouro-*.html renderRoadmap). Source = ROADMAP.md (REWRITTEN 2026-06-30 around the 3 GATES: bootstrap floor + GATE 1/2/3 + write-access unlock) + TODO ACTIVE. `name` fields match the ROADMAP ### headers so the overlay lines up with the _plan payload (same order). Each item has: `what` (plain: what it IS), `note` (why done/not-done), `conditions` (the gate criteria that must be MET to call it finished â€” each R/Y/G), and `tasks` (the work items â€” each R/Y/G + owner). Keep honest (RULE 5 #2). Status values: done|in_progress|planned -> color_map.
roadmap_status.we_are_here_note: The modern structure is live and PRODUCING: [3.70] invention cycles hands-off (3 machine-minted experts REGISTERED at held-out 1.0; list+boundary+composed master via fold/param-fold/MultiFold, zero new atoms); write-gate = honest 0/35 STRICT (Gabriel-ruled) with a visible progress metric building; [3.60] planner data-gated on trajectory accrual.

stages.name: The bootstrap floor â€” the ground the core stands on
stages.what: The minimal ground the core builds FROM: run / write / execute-verify / deploy. The operator core built as a ROUTER + a library of trained EXPERTS + an execution-grounded escalation ladder (judge + write + propose), the gap-filling engine (decompose -> build experts -> gradient via an execution checker), and a durable distributed cluster. Was Stage 0 (substrate) + Stage 1 (operator-capable core) in the old framing.
stages.note: BUILT + running unattended. ~97% live verified-rate on what the loop poses; the field/recompute families are EXACT (any difficulty); the two named walls are DOWN (numeric via limbed; monolithic write/judge via judge-as-recompute). The NN-architecture-search 'brain' is intentionally PAUSED as background substrate (no longer the north-star path). Keep this floor working; everything above it, the core builds.

run / write / execute-verify / deploy floor operational + durable cluster

stages.name: GATE 1 â€” Know its capabilities
stages.what: A real self-model over its own library: for any task, 'can I do this? what do I cover, at what mastery, with what confidence?' Introspection over the built expert set, grounded in REAL coverage data (judge_coverage scans + the manifest), NOT hand-authored.
stages.note: CLOSED 2026-06-30 â€” all 6 items done + live. Self-model built ([1.01]) -> queried ([1.02]) -> live coverage display ([1.03], n_families 15/11 solvable/mean_cov 0.76) -> persisted ([1.04]) -> wired into self_diagnose ([1.05], 4/4) -> feeds the honest core_score ([1.06] (c) realcap_d): core_score ~30 -> ~586 (crediting genuine d=647 capability, not [3.00]-capped posed-d). Gate-1 CAPABILITY complete.

stages.name: GATE 2 â€” Know what a task needs
stages.what: Decompose an arbitrary task -> identify the missing primitive/expert it lacks = the gap-filling CLOSURE. The three meta-capabilities that make coverage self-expanding: auto-CHECKER (the keystone: for any task, build an execution-grounded checker = the gradient), auto-POSER (generate instances + difficulty), auto-FAMILY-INVENTOR (propose a new domain = generator + checker). Bootstrapped by the escalation ladder (a capable rung performs each meta-task first, then distills down).
stages.note: DIRECTIVE #1 COMPLETE 2026-07-02 (the milestone): "write 500 digits of pi in the notepad" ran END-TO-END on the hypernet loop -- decompose -> route -> structure-search honest-fail (0.170) -> the FIRST LIVE FAILURE-DRIVEN REPLAN (tool-use: VM python/mpmath, Gabriel-ruled) -> GUI type on :99 -> verify (file EXACT vs the independent Chudnovsky checker + prefix-consistent screen) -> n_gaps=0. The verified trajectory (incl. the replan) logged = [3.60] planner training data. DIRECTIVE #2 issued ("make all 500 digits visible on screen" -- method = the system's discovery; replan machinery just proven). Banked from #1: the iteration operator, the structure-family reach-map, tool-use compute, tri-state perceptual verify. Queue: [3.60] planning-as-a-task (gate now OPEN pending #2), [3.70] in-loop invention (design).

stages.name: GATE 3 â€” Know how to implement it: AUTHOR + WIRE arbitrary code
stages.what: AUTHOR new code / a new subsystem from an NL directive + WIRE it into the running system itself. Greenfield authoring (distinct from self-repair + pure-function families): interpret a directive -> build plan -> author a new multi-file subsystem -> wire it into the running app -> verify via an INTEGRATION/OUTCOME check ('it actually speaks'). The 'give yourself a voice' benchmark.
stages.note: author+wire MACHINERY BUILT 2026-07-01: all 5 modules (decompose [3.03]/author [3.01]/inject [3.02]/check [3.04]/STEP-3.5 orchestrator [3.06]), ~28 tests. **RULE 5 #2 CAVEAT: E2E-tested with a MOCK writer â€” the REAL capability (the core authors + wires a WORKING subsystem from a real directive, end-to-end) is NOT yet verified.** Activation + live-7B E2E + distill remain. Machinery != capability; do NOT call GATE 3 done until a real live-7B author->wire->works is demonstrated. NOTE (Gabriel 2026-07-01): GATE-3 verification = "author a working subsystem, real E2E" â€” NOT 7B-specific. The current author IS the 7B = a transitional BOOTSTRAP crutch (same deprecation path as the escalation ladder); the clean end-state authors via DECOMPOSED write-experts (flywheel-decomposition), no 7B.

stages.name: THE UNLOCK â€” write access -> the hands-off flywheel
stages.what: Once the 3 gates hold, UNGATE write access (behind a checkpoint + auto-revert rail). The core then builds all its own infrastructure (screen-reading, research, supervisors, watchdogs, self-deploy) autonomously = the hands-off flywheel: self-diagnosis, self-posed work, owned compute/economic rails (crypto->compute, human-owned + capability-gated), compounding. 'Plug it in, come back later to a vastly self-improved system.' Was Stage 3.
stages.note: CONDITIONS SET (Gabriel 2026-07-02, verbatim in seed_store/unlock_conditions.md): (1) escalation rate 0 across the last 35 iters; (2) then the READ-ONLY repo-improvement test (whole-repo read + improve-the-repo directive -> a DIFF, never applied -> adversarial fleet review at extreme depth/breadth -> clean -> GABRIEL final review -> he alone unlocks). Operates UNDER the primary directive list (#1 dominates). The test doubles as the GATE-3 verification on the own repo.
